MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nilu K. Majd, Esq. of COOLEY MANION JONES HAKE KUROWSKI LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendants / moving party Collins Electrical Company, Inc.
Eric Solomon, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John Burns

RULING:
DEFENDANT COLLINS ELECTRICAL COMPANY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Burns was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant SVI Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Stockham or 'Stockholm' valves) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. Burns' declaration are overruled. The court gave the benefit of the doubt to plaintiff and has treated all references in plaintiff's deposition to 'Stockholm' as referring to 'Stockham.' Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted.
